title: Hedera marginata-rubra
taxonomy: Hedera marginata-rubra is a cultivar of the Hedera genus, which is part of the family Araliaceae. This family includes a large group of flowering plants commonly known as ginsengs, ivies, and sarsaparillas. Hedera marginata-rubra is a type of ivy that is known for its unique red or purple-tinged leaves. As a cultivar, it is a human-made variation of the species Hedera helix, which is the most widely recognized species in the genus. The classification of Hedera marginata-rubra reflects its origins as a cultivated variety of the common English ivy.
uses: Hedera marginata-rubra, also known as the red-leafed English ivy, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling. In some cultures, a tea made from the plant's leaves is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
